userNote: `editEntry(categoryId, index)` (line 251) is a stub that only `console.info`s the args. There is no form to set a `models` entry's provider / model / apiKey / baseUrl / enabled fields. `addEntry()` (line 243) only pushes the placeholder `{id:'new-entry', name:'New Entry'}` and never asks the user for the actual schema. Combined effect: the entire LLM_PROVIDERS registry is plumbing with no user-facing surface — a user cannot create a usable `models` entry from the UI today.
actionsTaken: Built real editEntry flow in src/frontend/components/settings/settings.js: (1) MODEL_ENTRY_SCHEMA declares editable model fields (id, name, provider, model, apiKey, baseUrl, enabled) with type + label + sensitive flag; (2) ENTRY_SCHEMAS map registers per-category schemas (extensible — only `models` today); (3) buildModelEntryFields(entry, schema) is a pure helper exported for tests, returns field descriptors with empty-defaults for missing keys and null-safety; (4) editEntry() validates category has a schema + index is in range, snapshots the entry to settingsState.editingEntry.snapshot, deep-clones into .draft, and calls _renderEditEntryForm which renders the form into #settings-main (replacing the list view); (5) provider field is a <select> populated by buildProviderOptions(f.value) — wires ticket 6 consumer; (6) apiKey field uses type="password" with autocomplete="new-password" + spellcheck="false" — masking is a hard security invariant verified by test; (7) updateEditField writes only to .draft (live entry untouched until save); (8) cancelEdit clears editingEntry and re-renders the list — does NOT POST (verified by test); (9) saveEntry optimistically applies draft to entries[i], persists the WHOLE array via _persistArrayEntries (POSTs {category, key:'_entries', value: array}), awaits the Promise<boolean> from _persistSetting (ticket 9 contract), and on false reverts the live entry + shows _showEditError inline; (10) loadSettings was extended with unwrapArrayCategory() to detect the {_entries:[...]} shape and unwrap to a bare array so renderCategoryEntries' Array.isArray branch still fires; (11) addEntry + duplicateEntry now also _persistArrayEntries so the persistence layer mirrors the in-memory array; (12) CSS for .settings-edit-actions and .settings-edit-error in settings.css. 12 new tests in tests/frontend/settings-module.test.js cover: schema shape, apiKey type=password + sensitive (security probe), buildModelEntryFields full+empty+null entries, editEntry no-schema warn, deep-clone draft isolation, updateEditField draft-only mutation, cancelEdit no-POST, saveEntry success path (POSTs _entries shape), saveEntry non-2xx revert (live entry restored), and unwrapArrayCategory round-trip through loadSettings.

userNote: No backend route exists to actually invoke a configured model. Even if `editEntry()` were built and a user populated a `models` entry with provider + apiKey + model, there is no `/api/llm-call` or `/api/chat` endpoint that consumes a `models` entry, routes to the right provider SDK, and returns a completion. The settings layer is feeding nothing.

`upsertSetting()` (line 622) stores `value` as plain TEXT in `st8_settings`. A `models` entry's `apiKey` is therefore persisted plaintext into SQLite (`st8.sqlite`). The LLM_PROVIDERS registry has an `envKey` fallback documented in the schema comment but the persistence path has no opt-out — once `editEntry()` ships, every typed-in key lands on disk in cleartext. Needs either env-only mode (refuse to store, read from `process.env[envKey]` at call time) or symmetric encryption at rest.
actionsTaken: Added src/shared/utils/settings-crypto.js — aes-256-gcm encrypt/decrypt with a 32-byte key stored at <dbDir>/.st8/encryption.key (mode 0600). Format: base64(iv):base64(authTag):base64(ciphertext). Wired into persistence.js via _encryptModelEntries / _decryptModelEntries: upsertSetting intercepts category='models' Array values and encrypts each entry.apiKey before INSERT; getSetting / getSettingsByCategory / getAllSettings symmetrically decrypt on read. Idempotent via isCiphertext() shape recognizer (verifies 3 base64 segments + IV=12 bytes + tag=16 bytes + canonical base64 round-trip). Non-models categories pass through. Corrupt ciphertext surfaces a '[decrypt-failed]' marker rather than throwing (one bad row mustn't break /api/settings). Test hook _getRawSetting() exposes pre-decryption row for the kickback-worthy probe. 9 tests in tests/core/database/settings-encryption.test.js cover crypto round-trip, isCiphertext recognizer, key file generation + mode 0600, persistence round-trip, RAW row contains ciphertext (NOT plaintext) — the apiKey literal does not appear in the raw row, non-models pass-through, idempotency under re-write, corrupt ciphertext sentinel, and getAllSettings/getSettingsByCategory decrypt paths. Live probe (sqlite3 against a fresh DB) confirms the on-disk apiKey field is the three-segment ciphertext form, not the plaintext input.

userNote: `SETTINGS_CATEGORIES` (line 15) and `DEFAULT_SETTINGS` (line 67) must be kept in lock-step manually. There is no assertion at module load that every category id has a matching DEFAULT_SETTINGS top-level key (or vice versa). A typo — `voidflow` vs `void_flow` — silently produces an empty form because `renderCategoryEntries` falls through to `{}`. Add a startup sanity check that compares `SETTINGS_CATEGORIES.map(c => c.id).sort()` against `Object.keys(DEFAULT_SETTINGS).sort()` and throws (or console.errors loudly) on mismatch.
actionsTaken: Added IIFE `_assertCategoriesMatchDefaults` immediately before the public-API block in settings.js. It compares `SETTINGS_CATEGORIES.map(c=>c.id).sort()` against `Object.keys(DEFAULT_SETTINGS).sort()`, console.errors the mismatch, then throws so the failure surfaces in CI + node-vm tests. Backed by tests/frontend/settings-module.test.js which loads the module in a vm sandbox and asserts (a) clean load on the canonical source, (b) throw when SETTINGS_CATEGORIES has an unmatched id, (c) throw when DEFAULT_SETTINGS has an unmatched key.
reviewNote: IIFE at settings.js:422 runs at module load (not lazy), sorts both key sets, console.errors then throws on mismatch. Vm-sandbox tests inject a real source mutation (extra category id, then extra default key) and assert.throws against the matcher — both are genuine probes.

userNote: No type validation on persisted values. `renderCategoryEntries` (line 129) infers the input type via `typeof value`. If the SQLite round-trip ever returns `reveal_wpm: "200"` (string) instead of `200` (number) — which can happen if a value was written before its key was numeric, or if JSON.parse falls through to the raw string branch in `persistence.getAllSettings` — the field renders as a text input and subsequent edits propagate strings. Validate against DEFAULT_SETTINGS' type for the same key before rendering and coerce or warn.
actionsTaken: Added `coerceSettingValue(categoryId, key, value)` + `coerceCategoryValues(categoryId, entries)` to settings.js. Coerces string→number, string→boolean, primitive→string, JSON-string→object against DEFAULT_SETTINGS' expected type for the same key. Garbage values (e.g. 'nope' where a number is expected) fall back to the default and emit a console.warn — no silent corruption. Wired into `loadSettings()` (after migration step) and `renderCategoryEntries()` (right before the typeof branch). 6 tests in tests/frontend/settings-module.test.js cover happy path + pass-through + fallback + array categories + unknown keys.
reviewNote: coerceSettingValue at settings.js:447 handles all primitive cross-type paths and falls back to defaults[key] + console.warn on impossible coercions ('definitely not a number' → 200 default, verified by test). Wired into both loadSettings() post-migration AND renderCategoryEntries() pre-typeof. All 6 tests are real probes.

userNote: `st8_settings` schema (line 132) has no migration story. If `DEFAULT_SETTINGS` ever renames a key (`reveal_wpm` → `reveal_words_per_minute`), the existing row stays under the old name forever and the new key reads as default. There is no `schemaVersion` column, no `_migrations` table for settings, no rename map. Same DB-migration gap as the broader codebase but bites harder here because the UI silently ignores orphaned rows.
actionsTaken: Implemented PRE_FLIGHT option (a) — lightweight per-key migration map. Added `SETTINGS_KEY_MIGRATIONS` ({'categoryId.oldKey':'newKey'}) and `migrateCategoryKeys()` to settings.js. The map runs at loadSettings() time before type coercion; renamed keys are re-persisted under the new name and the old key is best-effort DELETEd (fetch failure is non-fatal because the next load won't surface the old key once the new one shadows it). The map is currently empty (no renames pending) — the registration site is ready for the first rename. Full schema-level migration framework (schemaVersion column, _migrations table, version-keyed migration functions) is correctly deferred to persistence roadmap P1.1. 4 tests in tests/frontend/settings-module.test.js cover empty-map no-op, rename-on-load, new-key-wins-on-collision, and map-exists shape check.
reviewNote: SETTINGS_KEY_MIGRATIONS at settings.js:513 is a plain map (currently empty placeholder). migrateCategoryKeys runs at load before coercion; collision handling preserves the new key. Tests patch the SRC to seed a migration and verify rename + no-clobber. Schema-level framework deferral to P1.1 is appropriate.

userNote: `window.St8Settings.getLLMProviders()` (line 373) is exposed but no caller in src/ invokes it. Batch 025 added it specifically so the (future) `editEntry()` provider dropdown would consume it — but until that dropdown ships, the API is a dead export. Either ship the consumer or document the exposure as intentional future-facing.
actionsTaken: Added pure helper `buildProviderOptions(selectedId)` to settings.js that walks LLM_PROVIDERS and emits `<option value=...>name</option>` HTML, marking the matching id as selected. The helper is consumed by editEntry()'s provider field (ticket 0) and is the live caller of the registry — the previously-dead getLLMProviders() public API now mirrors the same source-of-truth. 4 tests in tests/frontend/settings-module.test.js cover (a) one option per provider, (b) selected attribute applied correctly, (c) unknown selectedId leaves nothing selected, (d) getLLMProviders() returns the same list buildProviderOptions consumes.

userNote: `DEFAULT_SETTINGS.theme` (line 84) declares `palette_overrides`, `font_sizes`, `spacing_scale`, and the UI happily renders + persists edits to them, but nothing in the front end actually applies these values to CSS. No `:root` style injection, no CSS variable bridge — the theme category is a write-only UI today. Either wire a `applyTheme(theme)` that sets CSS custom properties on `document.documentElement` after `loadSettings()` resolves, or temporarily hide the category until the bridge exists.
actionsTaken: Audit decision: outcome (b) — dormant but planned. Confirmed via `grep -rn 'palette_overrides|font_sizes|spacing_scale|applyTheme' /home/user/st8/src/` that the only matches are the declaration site itself. The full applyTheme() wiring is already on the cluster roadmap as P2.3 (docs/_pending-roadmap/settings-and-providers.md line 60). Added an inline block comment above `theme:` in DEFAULT_SETTINGS that (1) documents the dormant state, (2) names the consumers grep proved are absent, (3) points to the P2.3 roadmap entry, (4) warns future maintainers not to delete the keys without first deleting the roadmap entry. Choice rationale: removing the keys would orphan persisted user values; option (a) wire-now is out of scope for this sub-wave (frontend CSS work, not settings work).
reviewNote: Outcome (b) comment block at settings.js:84-97 cites roadmap P2.3 specifically; verified docs/_pending-roadmap/settings-and-providers.md line 60 contains '### P2.3 Apply theme tokens'. Pointer is live, deletion-warning is present.

userNote: `_handleSettings` (line 430) accepts any non-empty `category` + `key` strings. There is no enum check against `SETTINGS_CATEGORIES` ids — a POST with `category: 'voidfloow'` (typo) succeeds and creates a permanently-orphaned row that no UI category will ever read. Either expose a known-categories list from a shared source-of-truth module on both ends or validate against `getAllSettings()`-discovered categories at write time.
actionsTaken: Added `ALLOWED_SETTINGS_CATEGORIES` (Object.frozen array of the 8 canonical ids) near the top of app.js with a comment that the frontend SETTINGS_CATEGORIES is canonical and this is a mirror. Updated `_handleSettings` POST branch to reject categories not in the allowlist with HTTP 400 + a JSON body `{error:'unknown settings category', category, allowed}`. Exported the constant so the drift test can verify mirror-fidelity. 7 tests in tests/core/server/handle-settings-validation.test.js: drift test (parses the frontend literal and deepEquals against the export), 400 on missing category, 400 on missing key, 400 + allowed-list on unknown category, 200 on known category, and a GET round-trip proving rejected categories do NOT create DB rows.
reviewNote: ALLOWED_SETTINGS_CATEGORIES is Object.frozen, 8 ids. Route returns 400 with {error,category,allowed}. Drift test regex first matches the SETTINGS_CATEGORIES block then extracts id: '<name>' only from within — tight. MUTATION PROBE PASSED: removing 'network' from the backend allowlist made the drift test fail (and the count assertion). Restored. Verified via real HTTP boot on ephemeral port.

userNote: `updateValue` (line 196) writes to `settingsState.entries` and POSTs to /api/settings, but never reads the server response. A 400 (missing category/key) or 413 (body > 1 KB — unlikely for a single value but possible for a large JSON blob) only logs to console — the in-memory state already diverged from what's on disk. Either roll back on non-OK response or surface a toast.
actionsTaken: Refactored `_persistSetting()` to return a Promise<boolean> (true on 2xx, false on non-2xx or network error). Rewrote `updateValue()` to (1) snapshot the previous value + whether the key existed, (2) optimistically apply the new value, (3) await the persist Promise, (4) on `false` revert state (delete the key if it didn't exist before, restore prev otherwise) and call `renderCategoryEntries()` so the UI reflects the rollback. Returns the Promise so callers/tests can await. Pairs with ticket 8: a POST to an unknown category now both fails the persist AND rolls the UI back instead of leaving divergent state. Manual smoke path documented; the behavior is browser-side DOM-coupled (renderCategoryEntries depends on `document`), so integration is covered indirectly via ticket-8 backend rejection tests + the type-coercion sandbox tests.
reviewNote: _persistSetting returns Promise<boolean> (true/false on res.ok/non-2xx/network-error). updateValue snapshots hadKey + prevValue, applies optimistically, awaits, reverts state (delete-if-new or restore prev) and re-renders. The revert wiring is real. Direct DOM-coupled e2e test is acceptably indirect since the backend ticket-8 rejection path is fully covered with real HTTP.
